title: Dog Limping After Falling Off Couch: Triage Guide & Next Steps
meta_desc: Is your dog limping after a fall? Learn why it happens, the urgency level, and how capturing photos or videos can assist your veterinarian.
content_html:
Understanding Your Dog's Limp

It is incredibly stressful to watch your dog fall off the furniture and start limping. A limp is a physical sign of pain or discomfort, indicating that your dog is reluctant to put full weight on a limb. This could be caused by a simple muscle strain, a sprain, or more serious issues like a bone fracture or a joint luxation. Even a low-height fall from a couch can cause significant impact depending on how your dog landed.

Urgency Level: Medium

A dog limping after a fall is generally classified as a medium urgency situation. While it may not be a life-threatening emergency if your dog is otherwise acting normal, they are in pain and should be evaluated by a veterinarian within 24 hours. However, if you see an open wound, an obvious deformity in the leg, or if your dog is unable to stand or is vocalizing in pain, you should proceed to an emergency clinic immediately.

How Photos and Videos Help Triage

When you contact your vet or an emergency clinic, having a clear photo of the limb or a short video of your dog attempting to walk can be extremely helpful. Visuals allow the veterinary team to see the degree of lameness and the specific leg affected, which helps them prioritize your pet's care. Try to capture the video at your dog's eye level while they are walking on a non-slip surface if it is safe to do so.

faqs.question: Can I give my dog human pain medicine like Tylenol or Ibuprofen?
faqs.answer: No. Never give your dog human over-the-counter pain medications. Drugs like Ibuprofen, Naproxen, and Acetaminophen are toxic to dogs and can cause organ failure or severe gastric ulcers.

faqs.question: Should I encourage my dog to walk it off?
faqs.answer: No, you should strictly limit your dog's activity. Restrict them to a small area or a crate to prevent further injury until they can be examined by a professional.

faqs.question: How can I tell if the leg is broken?
faqs.answer: Signs of a fracture include a limb that looks a different shape than usual, rapid swelling, heat, or the inability to bear any weight at all. Only an X-ray can definitively confirm a break.
